aboutProfessionalObjective:
An environmental professional with 3+ years of experience supporting youth 
engagement, civic participation, and community-driven development initiatives in Nepal. 
Proven ability to work with local governments, civil society organizations, and youth 
networks to design and implement inclusive programmes, facilitate policy dialogue, and 
translate community voices into actionable governance outcomes. Strong background in 
results-based management (RBM), monitoring and evaluation, and the use of data and 
digital tools to support evidence-based decision-making. Experienced in working with 
marginalized communities, youth leaders, and municipal institutions across multiple 
provinces
bio: I am an environmental scientist specialising in water resource management, restoration ecology, and climate-resilient infrastructure. My work spans hydrologic modelling, contaminant fate and transport, and integrated urban water systems — with a focus on communities and rivers in the Hindu Kush Himalayan region.
name: Diwakar Adhikari
researchInterests: Integrated & Urban Water Systems, Governance and Political Economy
Modelling Water Systems for Climate-Resilient Infrastructure and Adaptation
aboutResearchObjective: I am an environmental science graduate student seeking to leverage experience in hydrological modeling, climate data analysis, and watershed‑scale environmental assessment to investigate hydroclimatic variability, human–environment interactions, and their interconnectedness within the Water–Energy–Food–Ecosystem (WEFE) nexus. Dedicated to generating science‑based insights that support sustainable water resources management, strengthen climate adaptation strategies, and enhance ecological and community resilience in data‑scarce mountain river basins.
